title: Document safe taskctl stale-lock recovery
summary: Document a narrowly scoped, approval-gated recovery procedure for an abandoned per-task lock after the same taskctl transition fails twice, without weakening active lock protection or adding automatic deletion.
acceptance: Workflow documents that the same lock failure must occur twice before stale-lock recovery is considered
Recovery requires confirming all task agents are inactive, the exact lock is inside coordination/tasks, the file is zero-byte/old enough, and no taskctl transition is running
Only the exact verified lock may be removed after explicit approval; globbing, recursive deletion and automatic lock eviction are forbidden
Active task locks remain fail-closed and taskctl transition semantics are unchanged
No product code, task state shortcut, protocol/schema/save/release change or unrelated workflow initialization

history.details: Scoped after two consecutive WM-0169 taskctl lock failures to documentation-only, exact-path, approval-gated stale-lock recovery; active lock semantics remain unchanged.

Documented two-failure, exact-path, 60-second quiet, approval-gated stale task lock recovery; active locks remain fail-closed, automatic/wildcard deletion is forbidden, taskctl implementation is unchanged, and all required checks pass.

verified: No blocking findings. The workflow requires the exact same taskctl transition and lock failure twice with a wait, then a 60-second quiet interval; all task worker/reviewer/director operations and taskctl transitions must be idle. Recovery validates the resolved absolute path as exactly repo/coordination/tasks/<TASK-ID>.json.lock, rejects traversal/symlinks/wildcards/out-of-directory paths, requires the current zero-byte lock format, records creation/last-write/current UTC times and proves the lock predates both failures, and rechecks expected pre-transition task state plus scoped worktree drift. Any uncertainty stops with a blocker. Only explicit user approval permits one Remove-Item -LiteralPath on the exact verified file, with no wildcard, recursion, computed list, multi-lock removal or automatic expiry; the original transition is retried once, and another failure blocks for a separate reviewed repair without manual state mutation. Source inspection confirms taskctl still creates the lock with openSync wx and retains fail-closed transition semantics. All required checks pass: node --check, validate-handoff 1643 files/176 tasks/10 roles, taskctl validate/status and diff-check. Commit cfc77b2..6d33c91 changes exactly the workflow skill/reference plus WM-0176 task/report; taskctl implementation and product/simulation protocol/schema/save/release files are unchanged.

singleWriterRule: Only project-director may edit this documentation-only workflow task; final review remains independent reviewer.
